creator: marklmi26-fbsd@yahoo.com
text:
(In reply to Brooks Davis from comment #2)

As far as I remember I used defaults for the options when prompted. I've only built devel/llvm37 when something else indirectly required it. The options file does date back to 2015-Oct-28 so my memory may be in question. But here the options are, however they got there:

# more /var/db/ports/devel_llvm37/options
# This file is auto-generated by 'make config'.
# Options for llvm37-3.7.0_4
_OPTIONS_READ=llvm37-3.7.0_4
_FILE_COMPLETE_OPTIONS_LIST=CLANG COMPILER_RT DOCS EXTRAS GOLD LIT LLD LLDB OPENMP
OPTIONS_FILE_SET+=CLANG
OPTIONS_FILE_SET+=COMPILER_RT
OPTIONS_FILE_SET+=DOCS
OPTIONS_FILE_SET+=EXTRAS
OPTIONS_FILE_SET+=GOLD
OPTIONS_FILE_SET+=LIT
OPTIONS_FILE_SET+=LLD
OPTIONS_FILE_SET+=LLDB
OPTIONS_FILE_SET+=OPENMP

Clearly it does list lldb as to be built.

If lldb (or any of the others) do not really make sense for devel/llvm37 it might be good for it to not have the option be selectable.

As for make.conf:

# more /etc/make.conf 
WANT_QT_VERBOSE_CONFIGURE=1
#
DEFAULT_VERSIONS+=perl5=5.22
WRKDIRPREFIX=/usr/obj/portswork
WITH_DEBUG=
WITH_DEBUG_FILES=
MALLOC_PRODUCTION=
